commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Consider and hear me, O LORD my God: lighten my eyes, lest I sleep the sleep of death; Augustine of Hippo 4. "Look on me, and hear me, O Lord my God" (ver. "Look on me," refers to what was said, "How long" dost "Thou turn away Thy face from me. " "Hear," refers to what was said," How long wilt Thou forget me to the end. Lighten mine eyes, that I sleep not in death. " The eyes of the heart must be understood, that they be not closed by the pleasurable eclipse of sin.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Enemy; Saul, or the devil. (St. Augustine) (Calmet)
